Title: Directionally bad. Issue #002

URL Source: https://deaination.substack.com/p/directionally-bad-issue-002

Published Time: 2026-05-23T09:17:09+00:00

Markdown Content:
Welcome back to the second issue of [DeAI Nation’s](https://deaination.com/) new weekly newsletter, _Directionally Bad_. Here, we talk about the risks of AI centralization and what gives us hope. Please spread the word if you like our work.

Without a doubt, the main event at the intersection of centralized and decentralized AI this week was Andrej Karpathy’s announcement that he’s joining Anthropic.

[![Image 1: X avatar for @karpathy](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oMwR!,w_40,h_40,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fpbs.substack.com%2Fprofile_images%2F1296667294148382721%2F9Pr6XrPB.jpg) Andrej Karpathy@karpathy Personal update: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R&D. I remain deeply passionate about education and plan to resume my work on it in time. 3:05 PM · May 19, 2026 · 26.6M Views 7.81K Replies · 11.1K Reposts · 148K Likes](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2056753169888334312)
Karpathy — a prominent AI researcher and educator, co-founder of OpenAI, and the creator of vibe-coding — is one of the most likeable people in the tech world (and for some, [god himself](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2056753169888334312) — no _The Boys_ finale pun intended). And he’s no stranger to open and decentralized AI: he was one of Prime Intellect’s angel [investors](https://www.primeintellect.ai/blog/fundraise) and a vocal [supporter](https://x.com/karpathy/status/1960803117689397543) of the startup’s crowdsourced platform for RL training.

Back in 2023, Karpathy [wrote](https://x.com/karpathy/status/1727033766252798272): “Thinking a lot about centralization and decentralization these few days.” Less than three years later, he has joined not just one of the centralized AI labs, but perhaps the most [hostile](https://substack.com/home/post/p-197930888) one toward open-source AI. Both OpenAI and Google have released open-weight models, while Anthropic is taking the opposite approach, with limited access to its frontier model, Mythos.

**What does it mean for the world?** Well, it’s complicated. Some, such as HuggingFace co-founders [Clement Delangue](https://x.com/ClementDelangue/status/2056774379418087453) and [Julien Chaumond](https://x.com/julien_c/status/2056758944333271410), and tiny corp’s [George Hotz](https://x.com/__tinygrad__/status/2056799109907169485), believe that Karpathy will convince Anthropic to be more open. Others were less optimistic.

Nathan Lambert, another AI researcher/educator and the post-training lead at the Allen Institute, [wrote](https://x.com/natolambert/status/2056759884952347026):

> Very happy for Karpathy. Also very lonely times in open science.

And in response to a comment saying that Andrej would hopefully stop sharing, Lambert [added](https://x.com/natolambert/status/2056770557157454134): “[D]on’t get your hopes up.”

[![Image 2: X avatar for @natolambert](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!wPPQ!,w_40,h_40,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fpbs.substack.com%2Fprofile_images%2F2051823244832083968%2FNr0pT8-V.jpg) Nathan Lambert@natolambert For a long time, academic researchers being at the cutting edge of new technologies has been a great social equilibrium. Neutral, unbiased technologists have been the people to spread new ideas to the world. As AI research takes off in velocity, it is also going behind closed 4:21 PM · May 19, 2026 · 66.3K Views 21 Replies · 40 Reposts · 374 Likes](https://x.com/natolambert/status/2056772229120295416)

6 DAYS REMAINING

Anyway.

[![Image 3: X avatar for @kyliebytes](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!6R42!,w_40,h_40,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fpbs.substack.com%2Fprofile_images%2F1981443668746625024%2FoLVdnSWa.jpg) Kylie Robison@kyliebytes ![Image 4](https://pbs.substack.com/media/HIsTcDebcAAk4da.jpg) 3:29 PM · May 19, 2026 · 2.5K Views 2 Replies · 97 Likes](https://x.com/kyliebytes/status/2056759030136373622)
Renting an Nvidia H100 GPU from [Nebius](https://nebius.com/ai-cloud) will become **up to 72% more expensive**. The neocloud has sent a letter to its customers notifying them that, starting June 1, GPU rentals will become 26% to 72% more expensive. It will cost from $2.15/hr to $3.85/hr to rent H100 depending on the conditions, up from $1.25-$2.95/hr.

The H100 is more than four years old.

Pluralis, a research lab with one of the most minimalist and sleek-looking sites out there, has released **Agora** — a technology that allows the use of consumer-grade GPUs for model pretraining.

[![Image 5: X avatar for @Pluralis](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!FAPt!,w_40,h_40,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fpbs.substack.com%2Fprofile_images%2F1901783277611917312%2FmmDxyPqC.jpg) Pluralis Research@Pluralis Today we're releasing Agora: the first ever pretraining stack that allows non-collocated consumer GPUs to be competitive with centralized clusters Agora is 15x faster than Megatron-LM in this setting and is only 1.5x less efficient in terms of tokens per unit compute than 4:25 PM · May 20, 2026 · 47.3K Views 17 Replies · 36 Reposts · 213 Likes](https://x.com/Pluralis/status/2057135718124904844)
As a pilot run, Pluralis [launched](https://pluralis.ai/docs/) the training of an 8B model using Agora. There are some limitations: the “consumer-grade” GPUs still need to be powerful enough and contain 24GB of VRAM, and the server must be located in North America.

Steffen Cruz, CTO and co-founder of Macrocosmos — a Bittensor-focused company operating its own model-training-oriented subnet, IOTA — [praised](https://x.com/macrocrux/status/2057783240937656330) Agora, writing:

> If this shit works, it will completely disrupt the economics of training large models and the floodgates will burst open.

Even if you don’t understand anything about decentralized training, just look at their [dashboard](https://agora.pluralis.ai/)!

[![Image 6](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4GYS!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3ccb3204-b433-43fa-a6b5-8cfb14059a2e_2808x1446.png)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4GYS!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3ccb3204-b433-43fa-a6b5-8cfb14059a2e_2808x1446.png)

Bittensor enthusiast Tensia Foundation has released its own version of [Cryptobubbles](https://cryptobubbles.net/), a visual representation of how major cryptocurrencies are doing at the moment. [Tensia Bubbles](https://bubble.tensia.foundation/) puts all the Bittensor subnets on the same screen in the form of bubbles representing their performance over a certain period of time.

[![Image 7](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!drtu!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F128b6d7a-68d4-40e3-bd53-723521f95d6f_2412x1430.png)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!drtu!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F128b6d7a-68d4-40e3-bd53-723521f95d6f_2412x1430.png)

Side note: can we leave the “bubbles” analogy to the centralized labs?

> The last few decades of technological progress democratized computing; but that era is now over.

[David Oks](https://open.substack.com/users/2088240-david-oks?utm_source=mentions), a research partner on the New Media team at a16z, published a great long read here on Substack, “[AI is killing the cheap smartphone](https://davidoks.blog/p/ai-is-killing-the-cheap-smartphone).” A sad prediction: consumer electronics will get more and more expensive. Thanks, AI.

That’s it for this week. Please, share your feedback with us by just hitting reply or contacting the author, Sultan Suleimanov, at sult@deaination.com. See you next Saturday!
